steps.title: Welcome to Day 25 — AI for Creativity & Innovation
steps.body: Creativity is not a mysterious gift — it is a skill you can practice. And AI is the best brainstorming partner you will ever have. Today you will learn how to use AI to generate ideas, break through creative blocks, prototype concepts, and refine your creative work.

steps.title: AI as your creative partner
steps.body: The best ideas often come from combining things that do not normally go together. AI is amazing at this. It has read millions of books, articles, and conversations. When you describe a problem, it can pull ideas from fields you never thought to look at. The key: bring your own taste and judgment. AI generates possibilities; you choose which ones have potential.

steps.title: AI brainstorming session

fields.label: What creative challenge do you face?
fields.placeholder: I need ideas for a birthday surprise on a small budget

fields.label: What are your constraints — time, budget, audience?
fields.placeholder: under $50, for someone who loves cooking and hiking

fields.label: Any style or vibe you are going for?

steps.template: Brainstorm creative ideas for: {{challenge}}. Constraints: {{constraints}}. Preferred vibe: {{style_preference}}. Give me 10 different ideas. For each idea, tell me: (1) The concept in one sentence. (2) Why it works given my constraints. (3) How I could execute it in under 2 hours. Be bold — combine things that do not usually go together.

steps.title: Save your best ideas
steps.body: Paste the brainstorm results. Highlight your top 2-3 ideas — these are worth pursuing.

steps.title: Creative block — Leon's wall
steps.body: Leon is a small business owner designing a new product. He has stared at a blank page for three days. Every idea feels reused and boring. He is stuck. AI breaks the block by asking different questions: 'What if your product was designed by a 12-year-old? What problem will this solve in 5 years? What would your competitor never try?' These fresh angles unlock ideas he could not reach alone.

steps.title: Idea expansion — making good ideas great
steps.body: You have a decent idea. How do you make it great? Ask AI: 'I have this idea: [describe it]. Help me expand it in three ways: (1) Add more detail. (2) Make it more unique. (3) Solve a hidden problem I have not considered.' AI can also combine your idea with entirely different concepts: 'Combine my idea with [random field — space exploration, ancient history, cooking].' The most innovative ideas often come from unexpected combinations.

steps.title: Creative process check
steps.question: What is the best way to use AI for creativity?
options: Let AI make all creative decisions so you do not have to
Use AI to generate many possibilities, then use your judgment to pick the best ones
Only use AI for logical tasks, not creative ones
feedback: Creativity needs human taste and judgment — AI is the generator, you are the curator.
Correct. AI floods you with ideas; your taste picks the winners.
AI can be highly creative — you just need the right prompts.

steps.title: Prototyping with AI
steps.body: Before you build something, test it with AI. Describe your idea in detail and ask AI to: (1) Find the weak spots. (2) Suggest improvements. (3) Predict how people will react. (4) Draft a simple version you could test in a day. Prompt: 'I am designing a [product/service/content]. Here is my concept: [details]. Play the role of a skeptical customer. Tear it apart constructively. Then help me design a simple test I can run this week to see if it works.'

steps.title: Design your creative project

fields.label: What creative project do you want to design?
fields.placeholder: a personal newsletter about local food spots

fields.label: Who is it for?
fields.placeholder: friends and colleagues who love food

fields.label: What format — written, visual, video, event, product?

steps.template: Help me design my creative project: {{project_concept}}. Audience: {{audience}}. Format: {{format}}. Help me: (1) Define the core concept in one sentence. (2) Outline the first version — keep it simple. (3) Suggest a name or tagline. (4) Tell me how to get feedback from 3 people this week.

steps.title: Save your project design
steps.body: Paste your creative project design. This is your blueprint to build from.

steps.title: Feedback and iteration — the creative loop
steps.body: Creativity is not a single flash of inspiration. It is a loop: create, get feedback, improve, repeat. AI is a tireless feedback partner. Prompt: 'Here is my [draft/prototype/concept]: [paste it]. Give me specific, actionable feedback. What is the strongest part? What is confusing? What is one thing I could change to make it significantly better?' Then iterate. Each round of feedback improves your work.

steps.title: Combining ideas — the innovation sweet spot
steps.body: Most innovations are not entirely new — they combine two existing ideas in a new way. Smartphones = phone + computer. Carpocalypse = car + apocalypse. Use AI to find combinations. Prompt: 'Combine [your field] with [unrelated field]. What new products, services, or approaches emerge? Give me 5 specific ideas.' The more unrelated the fields, the more innovative the results.

questions.question: What is the role of human judgment when using AI for creativity?
options: Human judgment is not needed — AI does the creative work
You generate ideas with AI, then use your taste and judgment to choose what works
Judge AI's output for grammar and spelling only

questions.question: How can AI help when you have creative block?
options: It tells you to take a break and come back later
It asks different questions and provides fresh angles you may not have considered
It completes your work without your input

questions.question: Innovation usually comes from entirely new ideas, not from combining existing ones.

steps.title: Day 25 complete — Creative Collaborator
steps.body: You can now use AI to brainstorm, break creative blocks, expand ideas, prototype, and iterate. Tomorrow: AI for time management and productivity — mastering your schedule and focus.
